chosen: Meta's global launch of Business Agent (free AI auto-responder in IG/WhatsApp/Messenger DMs), interpreted through the gap it leaves open: no CRM, no lead routing, no human handoff for hot leads
rejected: Instagram algorithm ranking signals (DM sends, watch time, saves) - already covered repeatedly across past posts
GoHighLevel calendar/service-booking edit feature - too platform-niche with thin actionability for a general owner audience
Google Business Profile Q&A discontinuation and messaging SLA - overlaps closely with a GBP response-time post already shipped

chosen: EZ Texting's 2026 Consumer Texting Behavior Report (959 US consumers): most people check a new text within 15 minutes and nearly 70% expect a reply within an hour, even from a business they've never used, reframed through Black Arrow's speed-to-lead lens with a this-week reply-speed audit.
rejected: SimpleTexting's aggregated 2026 SMS stats on check-time speed (lower-rigor blog aggregation vs. a fielded annual survey, more redundant with prior HubSpot post)
Numa's 'up to 80% of callers choose texting over voicemail' stat (self-reported customer data from a competing phone-answering vendor, less citable)
A meta post about carousel-vs-reel format performance (useful for internal strategy, not a usable tactic for the audience)
Older Twilio/ZipWhip texting-preference data from 2016-2020 (stale, not current per today's research standard)
